stories.title: Iran expert says Trump's 'war of choice' has morphed into a 'war of necessity'
stories.description: With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, policy expert Karim Sadjadpour says the war in Iran is becoming increasingly complicated: "I don't think President Trump ... understood what he was getting into."

stories.title: Trump lashes out at NATO after countries decline to help in Iran
stories.description: President Trump says NATO is making "a very foolish mistake" after countries decline to help secure the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Human rights advocates: War with Iran could spark new age of nuclear proliferation
stories.description: Could the war with Iran lead to a world with more nuclear weapons, not less? Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Zeid Raad Al Hussein, about a potential new age of nuclear proliferation.

stories.title: Joe Kent, a top counterterrorism official, resigns citing Iran war
stories.description: Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."

stories.title: U.S. seeks NATO help with Strait of Hormuz. And, federal judge blocks vaccine changes
stories.description: As the war with Iran intensifies, Trump is demanding that allies help the U.S. reopen the Strait of Hormuz. And, a federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies.

stories.title: A Trump official quits over the Iran war, as Israel says it killed 2 Iranian commanders
stories.description: Israel says it killed Ali Larijani and Gholamreza Soleimani. Iran has yet to confirm but it would be the highest-profile killings since the targeting of Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Israel's Lebanon incursion raises pressure on West over Iran-backed Hezbollah
stories.description: Western countries fear Israel's ground offensive in Lebanon aims to force them to act on disarming Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah, increasing tensions and urgency for a diplomatic solution.

stories.title: Historian shares how Russia benefits from the US being at war with Iran
stories.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with historian Sergey Radchenko about how Russia benefits from the U.S. being at war with Iran.

stories.title: Surge in US gas prices deepens political peril for Trump over Iran
stories.description: The odds of a quick resolution to the war appear to be dwindling by the day, and the political stakes for the president are only growing.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: Israel says 'limited' ground operations under way in Lebanon
stories.description: Troops are targeting "Hezbollah strongholds" in south Lebanon, where strikes reportedly killed nine people on Monday.

stories.title: How might the UK support shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Ben Chu from BBC Verify has been looking at what role the Royal Navy could play in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Two charged in death of anti-Iranian regime activist in Canada
stories.description: Police say the motive remains under investigation but noted the three had disputes on social media.

stories.title: Who wants what from the Iran war?
stories.description: Most people, although not everybody, want this war to end as quickly as possible. But on what terms?

stories.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
stories.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

stories.title: Iran hits key UAE oil port and Dubai airport
stories.description: The port of Fujairah plays a crucial role in helping keep global supplies moving when the Strait of Hormuz is blocked.

stories.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
stories.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

stories.title: Iranian footballer says 'everything will be fine' as she trains with Australian team
stories.description: Several players were granted Australian visas after concerns the team would face repercussions at home.

stories.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
stories.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

stories.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
stories.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

stories.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
stories.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Iran using cluster munitions in retaliatory strikes on Israel
stories.description: Iran is continuing its retaliatory strikes against Israel, with some damage to homes in central Israel this Tuesday. Israel says Tehran is using cluster munitions, which explode mid-air and indiscriminately scatter bomblets across a wide area. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.

stories.title: Top Iranian officials killed in Israeli airstrike in wartime blow to country's leadership
stories.description: Iran has confirmed the deaths of two senior security officials, considered among the most powerful figures in the country, in overnight strikes: Ali Larijani, secretary of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council, and General Gholamreza Soleimani, head of the Revolutionary Guard’...

stories.title: Iran confirms security chief Ali Larijani killed, state media says
War in Iran exposes cracks in Trump's MAGA coalition
stories.description: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, citing his concerns about the justification for military strikes in Iran and saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war. Kent, a former Green Beret...

stories.title: War on Iran: As top leaders and commanders are killed, 'regime will harden and close ranks'
stories.description: As Israel announces it has killed Iran's security chief, Ali Larijani, Annette Young is joined by Negah Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London. In assessing the reported killing of senior Iranian leadership figures, Ms. Angha argues that such developments represent a ser...

stories.title: Larijani was 'key to Iran's reach in the Gulf region'
stories.description: Reporting from Doha for FRANCE 24, Hoda Abdel-Hamid explains that Iranian security chief Ali Larijani "was key to Iran's reach to the Gulf region" adding that "the GCC and Iran were two parties deeply suspicious of each other, it took decades for them to get close to each other a...

stories.title: Trump says NATO's refusal to help on Iran is "very foolish mistake"
stories.description: US President Donald Trump lashed out Tuesday at "foolish" NATO over Iran, saying the United States needs no help after allies rebuffed his calls to join efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. FRANCE 24's international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani explains.

stories.title: Indian tankers carrying LPG arrive at port after safe passage through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Two Indian-flagged tankers together carrying over 92,000 metric tonnes of liquefied petroleum gas have arrived at ports in Gujarat state after safely passing through the Strait of Hormuz. New Delhi has been negotiating with Iran, but the government denies discussing the possible ...

stories.title: Fearing Israeli strikes, a Druze village screens for Hezbollah militants among Lebanon’s displaced
stories.description: In the predominantly Druze city of Aley, located on Mount Lebanon, authorities scrupulously check the identity documents of the many displaced who seek refuge there. They hope the measures will reassure a population fearful of inciting Israeli strikes over the potential presence ...

stories.title: REPLAY: Trump accuses NATO of 'foolish mistake' over Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday said the United States ​had been informed by most of its NATO allies that they did not want to get involved with the country's military ​operation ‌in Iran, a move he described ⁠as a "very foolish mistake". Trump's remarks came as he hosted Irish...

stories.title: 'Misguided' military operation: Trump’s appeal for Hormuz support draws no commitments from allies
stories.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Peter Trubowitz, Professor of International Relations and Director of the Phelan US Centre at the London School of Economics. According to Professor Trubowitz, what we are witnessing is a dynamic in which both sides are trying to expand the scop...

stories.title: 'I tell my children it’s just thunder': How ordinary Iranians are dealing with war
stories.description: As US and Israeli airstrikes continue to pound Iran, many residents there live in fear, choosing to limit outings as much as possible. France 24's Carys Garland and Ludovic de Foucaud take a look at how one family are trying as best they can to continue life as normal amid the co...

stories.title: Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war
stories.description: The director of the U.S. National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, has announced his resignation, saying he “cannot in good conscience” back the Trump administration’s war in Iran. Kent said on social media that Iran “posed no imminent threat to our nation, and it is clear that...

stories.title: Trump says US does not ‘need’ Strait of Hormuz help, despite appeal
stories.description: US president strikes defiant tone as allies reject joining coalition, does not name countries he says 'on their way'.

stories.title: Who was Ali Larijani, Iran’s powerful insider?
stories.description: Iran has confirmed that security chief Ali Larijani has been killed.

stories.title: FIFA rejects Iran’s request to move World Cup matches from US to Mexico
stories.description: The 2026 World Cup matches will be played as per schedule announced last year, the football organisation says.

stories.title: Could the Iran war keep Netanyahu in power for longer?
stories.description: Huge support in Israel for joint US bombing campaign in Iran.

stories.title: Iraq’s capital Baghdad rocked by powerful blasts near US embassy
stories.description: The attacks are part of an escalating cycle of violence between US forces and Iraqi groups aligned with Tehran.

stories.title: Trump says it’s a ‘good thing’ counterterrorism director resigned over Iran
stories.description: US President Trump reacted to the resignation of the US National Counterterrorism Centre’s director, Joe Kent.

stories.title: Starmer, Zelenskyy urge ‘focus’ on Ukraine as Iran war diverts attention
stories.description: As leaders of Ukraine and UK meet, Turkiye's top diplomat reiterates his country's offer to host Russia-Ukraine talks.

stories.title: Video: Man rescued from under rubble after attack in Iran
stories.description: A man was rescued from under the rubble of a destroyed building in Iran following an Israeli strike.

stories.title: Why Iranians are taking to Tehran’s streets during war
stories.description: As bombs fall and millions are displaced, protests grow in Iran. Could this war be strengthening Iran’s resolve?

stories.title: Iranian president vows to avenge death of Larijani
stories.description: Earlier, Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that any country allowing its territory to be used by the US or Israel for aggression against Iran "is a legitimate target for Iran"

stories.title: Iranian authorities confirm death of Larijani, appoint successors
stories.description: The politician died due to the US and Israeli strikes on Iran

stories.title: Israeli army no longer needs political approval to eliminate senior Iranian officials — TV
stories.description: According to the TV channel, this will make it possible to speed up the decision-making process

stories.title: UAE may join US efforts to protect Strait of Hormuz — Reuters
stories.description: Diplomatic adviser to the UAE President Anwar Gargash also said that once the US-Israeli war with Iran ends, an arrangement would be needed to ensure that Iran cannot use its nuclear, missile or drone programs "to terrorize the region"

stories.title: US spending on war with Iran could exceed trillion dollars — The Intercept
stories.description: The Pentagon said the US spent about $11.3 billion in just one week of its war on Iran

stories.title: Trump says could have decided to withdraw from NATO without Congress
stories.description: The US leader added that he was not currently raising the issue of withdrawing from NATO

stories.title: Lavrov, Fidan discuss Iran war, energy projects — Russian Foreign Ministry
stories.description: Special attention was drawn to the absolute importance of ensuring comprehensive pipeline safety

stories.title: German subsidiary of Russian pharma company announces bankruptcy due to sanctions
stories.description: According to the report, the main cause of the bankruptcy were European sanctions against Russia and Russian tit-for-tar measures, which adversely affected the business

stories.title: US allies reluctant to get involved in operation against Iran — Trump
stories.description: The United States does not need the help of anyone, the US president noted

stories.title: Trump says Europe unwilling to help US with Iran after American support regarding Ukraine
stories.description: The US president met with Irish Prime Minister Micheal Martin at the White House

stories.title: Iranian soldiers write messages on missiles telling US to get out of Middle East
stories.description: Iranian soldiers write on misseles that they will continue strikes until the last American soldier leaves the Middle East

stories.title: France to never participate in operations to reopen Strait of Hormuz — President Macron
stories.description: Paris will not do this under any circumstances, Emmanuel Macron said

stories.title: Pentagon says Iran's space program "destructed"
stories.description: Iran now appears to have no way of developing an intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) capable of reaching the United States, head of the Northern Command of the US Armed Forces, General Gregory Guillot said

stories.title: Slovak PM Fico predicts worsening global oil market situation amid Iran conflict
stories.description: There is every indication that this conflict will continue for a long time, Robert Fico noted

stories.title: Kenya may face fertilizer shortage due to conflict in the Middle East — radio station
stories.description: About 26% of all imported fertilizers are coming to Kenya via the Strait of Hormuz, the radio station said
